title: ObjectProxy.__new__ returns half-built proxy when PyDict_New() fails
author: GrahamDumpleton
body:
## Summary

The C implementation of `ObjectProxy.__new__` (`WraptObjectProxy_new` in
`src/wrapt/_wrappers.c`) does not check the return value of `PyDict_New()`
when initialising the proxy's instance dict. If the allocation fails,
`PyDict_New()` returns `NULL` and sets a `MemoryError`, but the constructor
stores that `NULL` into `self->dict` and returns the proxy to the caller as
if construction had succeeded.

## Credits

Thanks to @devdanzin for the report via:

* https://gist.github.com/devdanzin/e1ac0c43dc4d4278ee82455da4859872
